short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Run-rate $0.63B in FY2025 Q4, +18.7% YoY — accelerating trajectory entering the next print.
Next-quarter consensus: $0.50B (-20.6% vs current Q) — set by 24 broker contributors.
EPS consensus next qtr: $0.70.
Next earnings: 2026-08-26 — primary catalyst for the short-term window.
short_term.takeaway: Going into the next earnings print, the setup is mixed — execution risk on the next guide is elevated.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $2B — in cyclical drawdown.
Sell-side: FY1 $2B → FY2 $2B (+23.6% multi-year top-line implied).
Forward EBIT margin: 28.0% — structural premium business over the long-cycle view.
6-quarter run-rate CAGR: +30.5% — the secular trajectory the market is pricing.
Sector thesis (Information Technology): Multi-year AI/cloud capex cycle remains the dominant secular theme.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if the next 2–4 quarters confirm a stabilization in trajectory.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · trading window)
bullets: Price $329.14 vs SMA50 $266.27 (+23.6%) — short-term trend is bullish (price holding above the near-term moving average).
RSI(14): 84.6 — overbought — mean-reversion risk.
MACD: 17.58 vs signal 11.77 — bullish crossover.
30-day return: +22.1% — directional bias entering the next 4 weeks.
Bollinger band position: 89% of band width (band width 39.5% — normal).
Near-term S/R: resistance ~$339.01, support ~$319.27. 56% of 52w range (low $185.01 → high $440.60).
short_term.takeaway: Short-term trader bias: long (trend + momentum aligned bullish).

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · positioning window)
bullets: Price $329.14 vs SMA200 $321.49 (+2.4%) — long-term trend is in **neutral**.
Moving average regime: Death cross active (SMA50 < SMA200) — institutional trend filter bearish.
1-year return: +74.1% — strong long-cycle leader.

90-day return: +5.3% — positive intermediate-term momentum.
long_term.takeaway: Long-term positioning: accumulate on weakness — structural trend supportive.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–45 DTE · front-month tactical)
bullets: Front-month ATM IV: 79.9% — elevated (premium-selling favored).
Put/Call OI: 1.46 — put-heavy hedging (institutions defensive).
30-day implied move: ±24.4% ($248.92 – $409.36) — the 1-σ range the options market is pricing.
ATM skew: -10.43pp — call skew (upside bid — bullish positioning).
Front-month OI cluster: $177.5 — likely magnet / pin-risk for expiry.
short_term.strategy: Sell 30–45 DTE cash-secured put at $313 (5% OTM). High IV (80%) + uptrend → collect premium with a willingness to be assigned at a discount. Roll if breached, otherwise let theta decay.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ DTE · LEAPS / structural)
bullets: Back-month avg IV (90+ DTE): 71.8% across 10 expiries.
Available long-dated expiries: 10 dates spanning 93–940 DTE.
90-day implied move: ±36.5% ($208.89 – $449.39).
393-day implied move: ±73.8% ($86.29 – $571.99) — the long-dated 1-σ range.
Sell-side PT vs options-implied range: consensus $300 (-8.9% upside). Compare to the 90+ DTE implied range above.
Term structure in backwardation (-8.1pp front→back) — near-term event premium; long-dated vol cheaper.
long_term.strategy: Sell 6-month strangle: short $296 put / short $362 call. Range-bound + high IV → premium-selling on both sides; profits if spot stays within ±10%; manage with rolls or assignment.
